front: Work: a five-book dialogue that divides nature into four categories based on whether entities create and are created, defining nature as universitas rerum
back: The Division of Nature / Periphyseon (John Scotus Eriugena)

front: Work: suppressed for pantheistic implications after claiming 'God is everything that truly is because he himself makes all things and is made in all things'
back: The Division of Nature / Periphyseon (John Scotus Eriugena)

front: Philosopher: produced the first complete Latin translations of Pseudo-Dionysius's Divine Names and Celestial Hierarchy using his rare knowledge of Greek
Philosopher: legendarily stabbed to death with pens by his students, according to William of Malmesbury, though this is probably apocryphal
Philosopher: succeeded Alcuin of York as leader of the Palace School in Aachen and served Charles the Bald during the Carolingian Renaissance
Philosopher: quipped 'only a table' when a king seated across from him asked what separates a Scot from a fool
Philosopher: Hegel wrote that 'with this philosopher, true philosophy first begins'; compared interpretations of scripture to colors in a peacock's tail
